Apply f0() to numeric columns of a data frame

Source: R/f0.R
df_f0.Rd

Convenience wrapper: returns a copy of df where every numeric column has been formatted via f0. Useful as a pre-processing step before passing a coefficient table to knitr::kable() or group_kable when the no-leading-zero style is in effect.

Usage

df_f0(df, digits = 3, keep_sign = FALSE, cols = NULL)

Arguments

df

A data frame.

digits, keep_sign

Passed through to f0.

cols

Optional character vector of column names to format. If NULL (default), all numeric columns are formatted.

Value

A data frame the same shape as df; numeric columns are now character.

On this page
